source_meta.dialect: Palestinian (Northern Gaza: Jabalia / Beit Lahia (Jabalian dialect))
source_meta.collection_context_en: Transcription of an Instagram video in which a displaced man describes psychological disorientation, economic exhaustion, and fear of death during forced evacuation.
source_meta.collection_context_ar: تفريغ لفيديو إنستغرام يصف فيه رجل نازح حالة التيه، العجز المادي، والخوف من الموت أثناء النزوح القسري.

approx_context.location: Gaza City (during forced displacement towards the south)
approx_context.brief_summary_en: A displaced man expresses economic exhaustion, separation from meaningful social bonds, and a state of disorientation marked by fear of death during evacuation.
approx_context.brief_summary_ar: رجل نازح يعبّر عن الفقر، فقدان الروابط الاجتماعية، وحالة التيه والخوف من الموت أثناء النزوح.

segments.text_ar: بنصيح على قلة المصاري، وعلى فراق الناس المْليحة… ومش عارفين حالنا وين رايحين… تايهين… والله تايهين.
segments.text_en: We are crying over the lack of money, and over being separated from the good people… We don’t know where we’re going… we’re lost… truly lost.

event_frame.adversity_description: Economic deprivation combined with forced separation from newly formed social bonds during displacement.
event_frame.response_description: Open expression of distress and explicit naming of disorientation and loss of direction.

affective_signals.justification: Repeated reference to being lost ('تايهين') alongside crying reflects psychological disorientation under forced movement.

dialect_markers.note: We cry - Gazan colloquial usage indicating crying aloud or vocal distress rather than literal shouting (often written the same).
Lack of money - Colloquial expression denoting economic incapacity, especially critical during displacement when movement depends on money.

cultural_markers.note: The good people - Refers to meaningful social relationships formed during displacement, not merely moral goodness.

religious_markers.note: By Allah - Used to affirm sincerity and depth of suffering rather than as a filler expression.

trauma_markers.note: Loss of clear spatial and future reference points.

segments.text_ar: يعني طالع… طالع يا عالِم نشوفه ولا ما نشوفه… يمكن نموت… يمكن يموت… يمكن أنا أموت… مش عارفين وين رايحين فينا.
segments.text_en: We’re leaving… leaving, and only Allah knows whether we’ll see each other again or not… maybe we’ll die… maybe he’ll die… maybe I’ll die… we don’t know where they’re taking us.

event_frame.adversity_description: Evacuation under constant threat of death with no clarity about destination or outcome.
event_frame.response_description: Verbal processing of possible death through repetition and uncertainty.

affective_signals.justification: Repeated references to possible death reflect heightened survival anxiety and anticipation of loss.

cultural_markers.note: Gazan/Levantine idiom meaning 'Only Allah knows' or 'who knows?', expressing surrender to uncertainty rather than addressing the world (يا عالَم).

discourse_markers.note: Sequential listing of possible deaths (self and others) reflects rumination under threat.

trauma_markers.note: Fear of death before its occurrence, common in forced evacuation contexts.

طالع: خارج من المكان الذي انا فيه الان

anonymization.notes: No replacements were required under the current manifest; this copy mirrors the source folder. Public-release bundle: reversibility metadata stripped for GitHub.
